pr.title: Dump asm to a separate branch
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Added a dedicated CI/container path for generating assembly dumps and publishing them to a separate dump branch, instead of bundling dump generation into the documentation/pages generation flow. The PR introduced a dump-asm Docker target, a dump-asm GitHub Actions workflow, package publishing support for the new image target, and root-anchored ignore rules for generated dump-related paths.
what_changed: Created `.github/workflows/dump-asm.yml`, a new workflow that runs on push and pull_request, pulls a `dump-asm` container image, runs it with the repository mounted at `/input` and `/tmp/output` mounted at `/output`, then uploads the generated dump as a GitHub Actions artifact.

Added a second workflow job, `push-dump`, that only runs on master/main refs, downloads the dump artifact, checks out the `dump` branch, replaces its contents with the generated output via `rsync --delete`, commits as `github-actions[bot]`, and pushes.

Added `.github/packages/dump-asm/entrypoint.sh`, which clones `/input`, links `/opt/mwcc_compiler` into `tools/`, installs Python requirements, builds with `GENERATE_MAP=1 MAX_ERRORS=1`, copies `GALE01.map` to `/output`, runs `tools/parse_map.py`, and uses `dadosod dol` with `build/map.csv` to emit the dump into `/output`.
Extended `.github/packages/build-linux/Dockerfile` with a new `dump-asm` stage based on `linux-base`, declaring `/input` and `/output` volumes and installing the new dump entrypoint.
Updated `.github/workflows/publish-packages.yml` so the package build matrix includes `dump-asm`, and added a test run for that image target using the same `/input` and `/output` volume convention.
Removed assembly dump generation from `.github/packages/gen-pages/entrypoint.sh`; gen-pages now remains focused on Doxygen/context output and no longer runs the full map/dadosod dump pipeline.
Changed `.gitignore` entries for `src/m2ctx.py`, `expected/`, and `dump/` to root-anchored forms: `/src/m2ctx.py`, `/expected/`, and `/dump/`.
smart_moves: Separated assembly dump generation from pages generation, reducing coupling between documentation publishing and decomp artifact production.
Used a dedicated container stage and package target for `dump-asm`, making the dump job reproducible and testable through the same package publishing workflow as other CI images.

Mounted the repository read-only at `/input` and wrote generated artifacts to `/output`, which keeps the workflow output boundary clear and avoids mutating the checked-out working tree during dump generation.
Copied the generated dump to a separate `dump` branch only on mainline pushes, while still allowing pull requests to produce downloadable dump artifacts for inspection.
Used `rsync --delete` when updating the dump branch so stale files are removed and the branch reflects the current generated dump output.
Kept the dump branch update serialized with a `concurrency` group named `dump`, reducing the chance of concurrent CI runs racing to push generated output.
decomp_lessons: For large generated decompilation artifacts such as disassembly dumps, prefer a separate generated branch or artifact workflow rather than committing outputs into the source branch.
Keep documentation generation and assembly dump generation as separate CI responsibilities; both may require a build context, but their outputs and failure modes are different.
A dump pipeline can be automated from a normal build by enabling map generation, parsing the map, and feeding the DOL plus map CSV into the dump tool.
When CI needs the CodeWarrior compiler in a containerized build, this PR used `ln -s /opt/mwcc_compiler tools/` before invoking `make`.
Root-anchor `.gitignore` rules for generated top-level directories when the intent is only to ignore repository-root outputs, not similarly named paths elsewhere.
naming_conventions: The new workflow and package target consistently use the name `dump-asm`.
The generated assembly output branch is named `dump`.
The workflow artifact containing generated output is named `dump`.
The container entrypoint uses `/input` for the source repository and `/output` for generated dump files.
The commit message for generated dump branch updates follows `Dump of ${{ github.sha }}`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Uses `MAX_ERRORS=1` during the dump build, likely to stop quickly on compilation errors while still exercising map generation; the diff does not include rationale beyond the command itself.
Runs `python tools/parse_map.py` to generate `build/map.csv` from the build map.
Runs `dadosod dol build/ssbm.us.1.2/main.dol -m build/map.csv -o /output` to produce the assembly dump.
Publishes the raw `GALE01.map` alongside the dump output by copying it into `/output`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify `.github/workflows/dump-asm.yml` for fixes to branch checkout, permissions, or artifact handling.
Search for changes to `dadosod` invocation or dump output layout after PR 1000.
Look for review or issue discussion explaining why dump generation was removed from `gen-pages` and moved to a separate branch.
Check whether the `dump` branch workflow required additional GitHub token permissions in subsequent PRs.
